rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF BINH HON NGUY
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR INSTRUCTIONS
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 8/17/26. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) No proposed order has been lodged or received by the assigned examiner. [LRSF 14.7 A. and 14.59 E.] (2) Notice was mailed on 3/5/26. The petition was not e-filed until 3/10/26. Notice is defective if mailed before the filing of the pleading requiring notice. [LRSF 14.14 A.] "Filing" means when the pleading requiring notice appears on the Court's register of actions (and becomes publicly viewable in decedent estate and trust cases). (3) Notice must have attached to it the procedures for how to make a remote appearance by Zoom and CourtCall, as well as remote public access, as set out at https://sf.courts.ca.gov/divisions/probate-court. [LRSF 14.1 C.6.] Please provide a proposed order and courtesy copies of the petition and any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 8/3/26. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. NB: Except for exhibits, each paper filed with the court must bear a footer in the bottom margin of each page, placed below the page number and divided from the rest of the document page by a printed line. [CRC 2.110(a).]

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF DARLEEN MIN LUM
rulings.Calendar Matter: Respondents' Tim Lum And Fuling Lum'S Notice Of Motion And Motion For Protective Order; Memorandum In Support Of Motion
rulings.Rulings: [Part 1 of 4] This tentative ruling is issued by Judge Russell S. Roeca. Before the court is Respondents Tim and Fuling Lum's Motion For Protective Order ("Motion"). The Motion was improperly brought without the required meet and confer process, and without substantial justification. The attempts to meet and confer occurred after the Motion was filed. Sanctions are awarded, as follows. On October 10, 2024, Christopher Bricker filed Petition to (1) Enforce Marvin Contract; (2) Confirm Decedent's Property To Christopher J. Bricker Pursuant to Marvin Contract and Probate Code Section 850; (3) Enforce Oral Contract to Make a Will; and for (4) Damages for Breach of Contract; and (5) Constructive Trust; Demand for Evidentiary Hearing ("Petition"). On November 27, 2024, Respondents filed an Opposition/Objection to the Petition. On April 23, 2026, the instant Motion was filed. Per the Notice of Motion and MPA, Movants seek protective orders for medical accommodations and interpreter services pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure section 2025.420. Movants seek an order: (1) Excluding entirely the noticed deposition of Respondent, Fuling Lum, currently scheduled for April 30, 2026; (2) Deferring the noticed depositions of Respondents, Tim Lum and Fuling Lum currently scheduled for April 29, 2026, and April 30, 2026; (3) Requiring that the deposition of Tim Lum be conducted with the following accommodations, based on recommendation of his doctor, dated October 21, 2025, attached as Exhibit A to the Motion: he respond to questions in writing, if necessary; any oral questioning be limited to 30-60 minutes maximum; the deposition be conducted remotely; frequent breaks be allowed at Mr. Lum's request; and a caregiver or family support person be permitted to attend; (4) Requiring that the deposition of Fuling Lum be conducted with the following accommodations: she not to participate in oral testimony at this time at all, based on recommendation of her doctor, dated October 21, 2025, attached as Exhibit B to the Motion; and she respond to questions in writing, if necessary. In the alternative, for deposition of Fuling Lum, the Motion seeks any oral questioning be limited to 30-60 minutes maximum; the deposition be conducted remotely; frequent breaks be allowed at Ms. Lum's request; and a caregiver or family support person be permitted to attend; (5) the deposition of Tim Lum be conducted with the assistance of a certified court interpreter fluent in Cantonese-Chinese and English; (6) the deposition of Fuling Lum be conducted with the assistance of a certified court interpreter fluent in Mandarin-Chinese and English; (7) Requiring the parties to meet and confer in good faith to select a deposition date and time, consistent with the accommodations ordered. The Opposition states Petitioner is not opposed to the accommodations requested as to interpreters, total time limits, the Respondents' son - who is also a party - being present on camera, and breaks, and provides a counter-proposal, at paragraph 15 of the MPA and in the Declaration of Koss. The Opposition also argues this Motion was brough prematurely and before Movant had undertaken meaningful meet and confer efforts. Petitioner has filed evidentiary objections to the Exhibits. Petitioner seeks $5,492.00 in sanctions, on the basis of Movant's failure to meet and confer properly, requiring Petitioner to file the Opposition to an unnecessary Motion. In the Reply to Opposition, Movants submit declarations and updated letters from their doctor regarding their ability to sit for an oral deposition. The Reply seeks, for the first time, sanctions in the amount of $6,440.00 on the basis of Petitioner's opposition to the Motion. This request for sanctions is untimely and is DENIED.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF DARLEEN MIN LUM
rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR For The Purposes Of Posting The Tentative Ruling
rulings.Rulings: [Part 2 of 4] A motion for a protective order regarding a deposition must be accompanied by a meet and confer declaration (see, Code of Civil Procedure sections 2016.040, 2025.420) A meet and confer declaration in support of a motion shall state facts showing a reasonable and good faith attempt, either in person, by telephone, or by video conference, to informally resolve each issue presented by the motion. (Code of Civil Procedure sec. 2016.040.) The record before the court does not show Movant engaged in good faith meet and confer efforts, in person, telephonically, or by video conference, to resolve each issue presented in this Motion, before resorting to the court's intervention. The court, for good cause shown, may make any order that justice requires to protect any party, deponent, or other natural person or organization from unwarranted annoyance, embarrassment, or oppression, or undue burden and expense. (Code of Civil Procedure sec. 2025.420). A party seeking a protective order bears the burden of establishing good cause for such an order. (See, e.g. Nativi v. Deutsche Bank National Trust Co. (2014) Cal.App.4th 261, 318.) The Motion, and the Reply in trying to shore up the initial papers' deficiencies, do not make a showing of specific facts justifying all of the relief sought - including that Respondents be excused entirely from being deposed, be deposed via written questions, or oral deposition be limited to 30 minutes total. "[A] litigant has the right to take a proper deposition, and to receive responsive answers to proper questions [citation] for the purposes of discovery or for use as evidence, or for both purposes [ ][S]ituations will seldom arise where an order that the deposition shall not be taken will be appropriate. Such an order may not be made except for "good cause," and a strong showing is required before a party will be denied entirely the right to take a deposition." (Beverly Hills Nat. Bank & Tr. Co. v. Superior Ct. In & For Los Angeles Cnty. (1961) 195 Cal. App. 2d 861, 864-67.) Respondents have filed a substantive opposition to the underlying Petition, and are parties to this action. As Decedent's parents, they presumably have relevant information regarding Petitioner's claims and their opposition to them. The record before the court does not support excusing their depositions entirely, or severely limiting the total time allowed. Movant has also not made a sufficient showing that Code-compliant deposition by written questions would be any less taxing or burdensome on either Respondent than tailored oral depositions. Unlike special interrogatories, there is no limit on the number of written deposition questions. There is also no requirement that the deposition questions be translated into the deponent's native language. The deposition questions must be answered before a deposition officer, and the process of noticed questions, cross-questions, and questions on redirect, if fully embraced by the parties, would leave the Respondents subject to the deposition proceedings for weeks if not months (see, Code of Civil Procedure sections 2025.330(e), 2028 through 2028.080).

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF DARLEEN MIN LUM
rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR For The Purposes Of Posting The Tentative Ruling
rulings.Rulings: [Part 3 of 4] Overall, Movants have failed to meet and confer in an appropriate manner and have not met their burden under the statute to justify the relief sought in the Motion. Petitioner's request for sanctions is GRANTED pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure section 2025.420(h). Respondents Tim and Fuling Lum and their attorney of record Quianli Yang, jointly and severally, shall pay to Christopher Bricker $5,492.00 in sanctions, within 30 days of entry of this Order. Nevertheless, insofar as the parties agree in the papers there should be some form of certain medical and interpreter accommodations for Respondents' depositions, the court exercises its discretion to make any order that protects any party from abuse of discovery and ORDERS as follows: 1. Counsel shall meet and confer in person, telephonically, or over video conference within the next ten days to determine deposition dates for each Respondent. An initial deposition date for each Respondent shall be scheduled and noticed for any mutually-available date within the next forty-five days. 2. Deposition of each Respondent shall be held over video conference. 3. The Respondents are each allowed to have one caregiver or support person of their choice present at the deposition. The caregiver or support person must remain on-screen during the deposition, exclusive of breaks. 4. The deposition of Respondent Tim Lum shall be conducted with a certified court interpreter fluent in Cantonese-Chinese and English. 5. The deposition of Respondent Fuling Lum shall be conducted with a certified court interpreter fluent in Mandarin-Chinese and English. 6. The deposition of Respondent Tim Lum shall be limited to four hours total, exclusive of breaks. Each deposition session, up to the total number of hours, shall be noticed and scheduled for at least one hour, although the session may be suspended by the deposition officer after less than one hour if medically necessary and, if suspended, the deposition shall be continued to the next mutually available date. Mr. Lum may take breaks during the deposition at his election, so long as there is no question pending when he takes the break. 7. The deposition of Respondent Fuling Lum shall be limited to three hours total, exclusive of breaks. Each deposition session, up to the total number of hours, shall be noticed and scheduled for at least one hour, although the session may be suspended by the deposition officer after less than one hour if medically necessary and, if suspended, the deposition shall be continued to the next mutually available date. Mrs. Lum may take breaks during the deposition at her election, so long as there is no question pending when she takes the break.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ALLAN M. BAIRD
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. The procedural issues have not been cleared since Jan, 2026 hearing: (1) The original Will has not been submitted, and the petition has not been updated to request admission of a lost will. Although the petitioner filed a declaration regarding the original will on 2/26/2026, it does not appear to support the petition. To proceed, the petitioner must submit the original Will if seeking its admission. If the petitioner intends to admit a lost will, an amended petition with the required details will likely be necessary. The Court will not resolve the issue for the petitioner (2) According to the supplement, Scott H. appointed Joseph B. and Cassandra S. as executors. The Court does not have clear authority to appoint the petitioner as executor when the named executor's written declination to act is not on file. Also, allegations of other persons that the executor contradicts the relief of this petition. Scott H's written declination to act must be filed, along with his nomination of other executors. (3) The petition does not name the trustee of the residual beneficiary trust, and proper notice to the trustee is required. The notice on file lists several beneficiaries, but addresses for Robert B., Cassandra S., and Marisa Leonis are missing. (4) The petition must clarify how the petitioner intends to address the conflict of interest resulting from their role as a creditor. (5) A full bond will be required. (6) No notices have been provided to Hodge, Joseph B., Cassandra B., or their counsel, Attorney Sun. (7) Petition #8 does not identify the heirs at law. While three relatives are listed as trust beneficiaries, the petition does not confirm they are the only heirs. (8) Petition #3 alleges that the estimated value of the property is less than $208,850. Since the above issues have not been resolved, the Court may infer that this probate proceeding is not necessary. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF The MOMENT LEGACY TRUST(JJM) DATED 12/18/2012, AS Amended TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Jason Moment'S Petition For: 1) Breach Of Fiduciary Duties And Surcharge; 2) Suspension And Removal Of Trustee; 3) Prohibit Use Of Trust Funds For Defense Of Action; (4) Request For Instructions Barring Further Redemption From Trust'S Capital Account; And (5) Request For Accounting [Probate Code 15642, 16000, 16060, 16400, 16420, 17200, 17206]
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. Please have the CASE NUMBER and the CASE NAME ready for the clerk. Parties appearing must check in for the hearing using their FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear in person or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. Please review LRSF 14.1. For Zoom appearance: meeting ID is 160 225 4765 and password is 514879. For audio-only telephone appearance via Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF BERNICE DE LA JARA
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Hearing for the sole purpose of admitting the will dated 4/21/2019. No appearance is required. The Court will waive notice to the two minors; however, the ages of minors should always be set forth and notice must also be given to the person having legal custody of the minor with whom the minor resides. Status hearing will be set on 10/26/26 for filing the I&A. If the I&A is not filed by 10/16/26, an appearance will be required on 10/26/26. Status hearing will be set on 12/20/27 for closing the estate. If a petition for final distribution or a report of status with a petition requesting additional time for estate administration is filed at least two weeks before that status hearing date, no appearance will be required on that date.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF DAVID ALLAN HYMAN
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Matter will be called to admit will. No appearance is required. Status hearing on close of estate will be set for 12/20/27. No appearance will be necessary on that date if either a petition for final distribution or a petition for approval of report of status of administration pursuant to Probate Code 12200 et seq is filed at least two weeks prior.
